beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee at home
beats.reasoning: Ellie starts her Monday with espresso and a clear head; full energy means she's ready for a busy day ahead.
Early commute from Mount Baker to First Hill/Pike Place; sunny weather makes the drive pleasant, and she wants to prep the stall before the morning rush.

beats.activity: Work at florist stall: arranging, restocking, serving customers
beats.reasoning: Morning shift before the FIFA match; Ellie sets up arrangements and handles the early-Monday customer flow, staying focused on her work rather than bar crowds.

beats.activity: Lunch break at Pike Place, quick bite
beats.reasoning: Ellie takes a break and eats locally at the market; she skips the USA vs Wales match (not her priority) and stays focused on work rather than joining downtown bar crowds.

beats.activity: Continue work shift: arranging flowers, customer service
beats.reasoning: Long afternoon shift covering the busy lunch-to-dinner window; Ellie prefers steady work over social scenes and keeps her head down while downtown buzzes with match watchers.
Evening commute back to Mount Baker; Light Rail closure (Westlake–U District 9am–3pm) is over, but Ellie drives anyway to avoid crowds after the match excitement.

beats.activity: Tend to urban garden, water plants in the sunshine
beats.reasoning: Perfect sunny evening (74F high) for outdoor gardening; Ellie unwinds in her garden after a full work day, staying true to her love of urban growing and peaceful routines.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat dinner at home
beats.reasoning: Homebody Ellie cooks a simple dinner in her Mount Baker kitchen, savoring the quiet evening and avoiding downtown crowds.

beats.activity: Listen to music, relax on couch with herbal tea
beats.reasoning: Wind-down evening at home; Ellie's nostalgic, introverted side emerges as she unwinds with music and tea, staying true to her homebody nature before bed.
Full night's rest after a steady work day; Ellie sleeps soundly and wakes refreshed, ending the day at home as required.

diary: Woke up early to the kind of June morning that reminds me why I stay in Seattle. The light was already golden when I showered. Commute was smooth—thankfully I left before the Light Rail shutdown hit. The market was buzzing from the moment I arrived; apparently everyone and their cousin decided today was the day to buy flowers. Between arrangements and the usual Monday rush, I barely noticed the USA-Wales game starting at the bars nearby. Grabbed a quick bite around the stall, kept my hands moving through stems and greenery. By afternoon my feet were talking to me, but that good kind of tired. Got home, spent an hour in the garden just watering and breathing. The sunshine on my face felt like the whole day's reward. Made a simple dinner, then collapsed on the couch with tea and some old Fleetwood Mac. Felt settled.
thought_process: The weather pulled me out of any Monday funk before it started—I think that's half the battle. The Light Rail closure actually worked in my favor since I'm always an early commuter anyway. I noticed the World Cup buzz downtown but didn't feel pulled toward it; the market energy was enough for me today. Customer flow meant less time to overthink, which I needed. By afternoon I was ready to be alone, so I let myself skip the after-work social stuff and just exist in my own garden. It's funny how a day that looks routine on paper—work, commute, home—can feel complete just because the light was right and my plants needed me.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
